sections.heading: Support by Cluster Tier
sections.body:
Atlas supports changing a cluster's region and cloud provider:

- Anytime on a Flex cluster as long as the new region is [supported for Flex clusters](flex-limits-config).

- Anytime on an `M10+` cluster.

sections.heading: Preferred and Electable Nodes
sections.body:
Each node in the preferred and electable regions can:

- Participate in [replica set elections](manual/core/replica-set-elections)

- Become the [primary](manual/reference/glossary) if the majority of replica set nodes
  are available.

Electable nodes in the cluster must total 3, 5, or 7.

sections.heading: Migration, Downtime and Performance Impact
sections.body:
Depending on the amount of data to migrate, migrations can take a
significant amount of time. To maximize availability for a replica set,
Atlas migrates one member at a time, starting with the secondary
members first and then the primary.

Migration can affect performance if your primary is already reaching
operational capacity: each newly migrated replica set member must
perform an [initial sync](manual/reference/glossary) from the primary, adding to the
operational load. Migrations can also affect performance if
[read preferences](manual/core/read-preference) are set to read from
secondaries: the replica set is down one secondary during the migration.

sections.heading: VPC/VNet Peering (AWS and Azure)
sections.body:
If you move a cluster out of a region that has a
[VPC/VNet peering](vpc-peering) connection, the moved
cluster can no longer use that peering connection to communicate with
servers in the peered network. Any other clusters with nodes
remaining in the original region can continue to use the peering
connection.

You can create multiple peering connections for each region, including
a new peering connection with the region that you moved a
cluster to.

> **Note:** Cluster nodes removed from a region can't inherit access rules
> from security groups (AWS) or allowed CIDR blocks configured for the
> peering connection. You must [configure IP Access List entries](add-to-access-list) to allow the moved cluster to communicate with servers
> residing in the peered network. Cluster nodes remaining in the
> region are unaffected.

sections.heading: Billing
sections.body:
If you change your cluster's [highest priority region](deploy-across-multiple-regions) or if MongoDB migrates oplog data to
a different region, you will be billed for storage in both the old and
new regions for the days following the region change. You must disable
continuous cloud backup and reenable it to prevent billing in both the regions.

> **Note:** If you disable continuous cloud backup, Atlas will delete the
> continuous cloud backup history.

sections.heading: Move a Single-Region Cluster
sections.body:
Use the following procedure to move nodes to a single-region
cluster.

Click Edit Config and select the cluster that you
want to move from the drop-down menu.

Alternatively, if you are already viewing the specific cluster,
click Configuration.

For Free clusters:

- You must also select a higher cluster tier. Atlas supports
  moving Free clusters only when you increase the
  cluster tier.

- The available regions are a subset of the total supported regions for any
  given cloud service provider.

sections.heading: Move a Multi-Region Cluster
sections.body:
Use the following procedure to move nodes to one or more regions in an
`M10` or larger multi-region cluster:

Click Edit Config and select the cluster that you
want to move from the drop-down menu.

Alternatively, if you are already viewing the specific cluster,
click Configuration.

For a given region, click the currently selected
Region and pick a new region from the drop-down list.
If you modify the Preferred region, the Atlas
cluster calls for one or more elections to select a new
[primary](manual/reference/glossary) in the selected region. See
Test Primary Failover for
instructions on testing your application response to a replica set
election before changing the Preferred cluster.

To change the number of nodes Atlas deploys to a given
region, increase or decrease the Number of Nodes
value. The total number of nodes across the Preferred
region and all Electable regions must be an odd
number.
